refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a defined packet loss limit. It is focused on what the system must achieve (support teleoperation with specified communication quality) without specifying how to achieve it, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss stays below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain perceived communication quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control of network quality parameters independently from other system functions, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and can be monitored effectively
Risk that dynamic adjustments may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that packet loss can be controlled primarily through parameter adjustments rather than hardware changes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency and jitter requirements alongside packet loss to ensure comprehensive communication quality for teleoperation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to ensure quality of service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and receive network status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical vehicle functions; network control would diffuse significantly outside.
Teleoperation depends on network quality but does not own network control; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it directly manages network parameters and quality assurance.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for network usage data may cause minor interface complexity.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation or Vehicle modules, some diffusion may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss targets defined by the customer. This directly supports teleoperation with the required communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for network usage data may cause minor interface complexity.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation or Vehicle modules, some diffusion may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate architectural packaging for the DP. Minor diffusion risks exist due to dependencies on Teleoperation and Vehicle modules, but these are manageable.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and adjusting transmission parameters through the Network Link interface, ensuring the packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, as it encompasses the network-related functionalities required to fulfill the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to minimize diffusion risk and clarify data dependencies.
Monitor and manage any overlapping network control logic with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion and maintain module cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification that the DP logically addresses the FR, the IM module has high containment and low diffusion, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Minor diffusion risks are identified but manageable with recommended revisions. Given the high safety criticality, the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with appropriate architectural packaging and verification feasibility, so further decomposition is not necessary at this time.

revise.why: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to minimize diffusion risk and clarify data dependencies, and monitor overlapping network control logic with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to maintain module cohesion.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module encapsulates all network-related functionalities required to meet the FR.
Dependencies on Teleoperation and Vehicle modules are limited and manageable.
open_risks: Potential minor interface complexity due to dependency on Teleoperation for network usage data.
Possible diffusion if network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation or Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Verification data showing packet loss metrics remain within customer-defined limits during operation.
